emergency_definition: panel showing supervisory fault at a hospital site

sections.name: Greeting
sections.prompt: Thank you for calling Bright Shield Fire Protection. How can we assist you today?

sections.name: Purpose
sections.prompt: What brings you to call us today?

sections.name: Collect name + number
sections.prompt: May I have your name and phone number, please?

sections.name: Route call
sections.prompt: I'm going to route your call to one of our specialists. Please hold for just a moment.

sections.name: Transfer fallback
sections.prompt: We are escalating this now and a fire protection specialist will call you shortly.

sections.name: Ask if anything else
sections.prompt: Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

sections.name: Close call
sections.prompt: Thank you for calling Bright Shield Fire Protection. Have a great day.

sections.name: Greeting (office closed)
sections.prompt: Thank you for calling Bright Shield Fire Protection. Our office is currently closed. How can we assist you?

sections.name: Ask purpose
sections.prompt: What is the purpose of your call?

sections.name: Confirm emergency
sections.prompt: Is this an emergency, such as a panel showing a supervisory fault at a hospital site?

sections.name: Emergency -> collect info -> transfer
sections.prompt: I'm going to collect some information and then transfer you to our on-call specialist. Can you please provide more details about the issue?

sections.name: Transfer fail -> reassure callback
sections.prompt: We are unable to reach our on-call specialist at this time. We will have someone call you back within 7 minutes.

sections.name: Non-emergency -> collect details
sections.prompt: We will collect some details and offer a callback slot for the next business day. Can you please provide your name, phone number, and a brief description of the issue?

sections.name: Close call
sections.prompt: Thank you for calling Bright Shield Fire Protection. We will have someone call you back soon.

call_transfer_protocol.message_if_fails: We are escalating this now and a fire protection specialist will call you shortly

tool_invocation_note: No tool invocation is required for this configuration.
